summary: Average‑sounding soundboard with recessed vocals and keyboards, but a strong set highlighted by the rare Cumberland→Samson segue, He's Gone and an inspired Peter performance.
review: The recording is a soundboard mix that sounds average – loud enough but with the vocal mix pushed back and keyboards almost buried. Musically the show shines, featuring a one‑time Saint → Cumberland transition into Samson, plus standout moments on He’s Gone, the perfect Smile line, and a soaring Peter rendition.

ai_rating.rationale: The performance is praised for many highlights (≈4/5) while the recording quality is only fair (≈3/5); weighted average yields a solid 3.7‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Average sounding; vocals and keyboards sit back in the mix, loud but not as punchy as other nights. Reviewer recommends audience recordings over this board for better balance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rare Saint → Cumberland segue followed by the only other Samson of the run; overall set flows well with strong vocal moments and tight transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Sings on several numbers (Smile line, Peter) with his guitar; praised for vocal delivery.
band_member_comments.Phil: Vocals are a bit recessed in the mix; no specific performance comment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Adds great flourishes but his guitar can drown Jerry’s vocals during the "Come and See" chorus.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards sit even further back than vocals, making them hard to hear.
